news.title: STAT+: French regulator fines Novo and Lilly over weight loss ad campaigns
news.summary: France's medicines regulator has fined Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly over their weight loss ad campaigns.

news.title: Opinion: Mifepristone court ruling makes drug development riskier for everyone
news.summary: The 5th Circuit’s ruling on mifepristone “is bad for health care access, and it’s also bad for innovation,” writes Grace E. Colón.

news.title: Supreme Court restores access to abortion pill mifepristone through telehealth, mail, and pharmacies
news.summary: The Supreme Court on Monday restored broad access to the abortion pill mifepristone, blocking a ruling that had threatened to upend one of the main ways abortion is provided across the…

news.title: Researchers urge study of paternal deaths, though a new paper finds fatherhood is protective
news.summary: A research letter published on Monday in JAMA Pediatrics argues that paternal mortality deserve more attention

news.title: STAT+: Pump the brakes on AI, buddy; and deposition deadlock
news.summary: It's tough to write nuanced headlines, but a new study on AI in emergency rooms is getting way overhyped.

news.title: UCB to acquire maker of antibody treatments for autoimmune diseases
news.summary: A second shot at gene therapy for Huntington's, China's tightening grip on pharma supply chains, and more Readout biotech updates

news.title: What to know about hantavirus, the illness suspected in a cruise ship outbreak
news.summary: A rodent-borne illness is suspected of causing an outbreak aboard a cruise ship that has killed three people and sickened others. Here's what to know about hantavirus.

news.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about an abortion pill controversy, another UCB deal, and more news
news.summary: Two drugmakers asked the U.S. Supreme Court to restore mail-order access to the abortion pill mifepristone, after an appeals court suspended deliveries

news.title: Federal court blocks mailing of mifepristone
news.summary: Longevity "blue zones," an update on uterine transplant science, and more Morning Rounds health news

news.title: STAT+: Biotech raises $42 million to run Huntington’s disease trial
news.summary: Latus Bio raised $42 million as it prepares to seek FDA approval to start a clinical trial for its gene therapy for Huntington's disease.

news.title: Opinion: STAT+: China’s strict new supply chain regulations could create massive problems for Western biopharma companies
news.summary: China is now a peer competitor in drug innovation — and it intends to stay that way on its own terms, writes Dennis Kwok.

news.title: Opinion: Are blue zones real? Answering that question is harder than ever
news.summary: "Blue zones" as a concept have strayed far and wide from their humble roots, write Shelley Wood and Eric Topol.

news.title: Immigration changes are driving foreign researchers to leave the U.S. — or not come to begin with
news.summary: Labs are seeing fewer applications from international students and researchers, and some doctoral students are considering leaving early.

news.title: American Hospital Association, West Health Institute partner to help health systems scale new tech
news.summary: The three-year initiative will prioritize EHR optimization, virtual care and AI utilization and integration for participating health systems and hospitals.

news.title: CommonSpirit locks in sale of Ohio-based Trinity Health System to UPMC
news.summary: The parties have progressed their talks to a definitive agreement that would give UPMC a foothold in the neighboring Buckeye State.

news.title: WakeMed Health's plans to join Atrium Health face swift pushback from NC officials
news.summary: A combination unveiled May 1 would bring WakeMed Health $2 billion in promised investment from the major nonprofit system. State officials voiced concerns about the impact of such consolidation and the value of those commitments, delaying a planned go-ahead vote by a county board.

news.title: Nearly half of reproductive age women with Medicaid coverage live in states restricting abortion: KFF
news.summary: Despite the expansion of abortion funding for Medicaid enrollees, nearly half of women of reproductive age with Medicaid coverage live in a state that follows the Hyde Amendment restrictions (29%) or has a law banning the provision of abortion (19%).

news.title: Alignment CEO expects short delay for CMS' proposed risk adjustment changes
news.summary: Alignment Healthcare executives weighed in on the final Medicare Advantage rate notice last week, with CEO John Kao saying he expects regulators to return to plans for a new risk adjustment model in short order.

news.title: SCOTUS temporarily restores online access to abortion pill after appeals court ruling
news.summary: The U.S. Supreme Court has temporarily reinstated online access to the abortion pill mifepristone after a federal appeals court in New Orleans ruled to prevent mifepristone from being prescribed over the phone and sent through the mail in the U.S. while a Louisiana lawsuit plays out.

news.title: Fierce Healthcare Layoff Tracker—Cuts at HCA Healthcare; Baptist Health laying off 150
news.summary: Stick with this tracker for the latest layoff and workforce updates from across healthcare.

news.title: The Case for a More Proactive Payment Integrity Program
news.summary: Spring is the season of renewals and hard conversations. If your payment integrity program hasn't kept pace with a shifting market, the gap between what you're paying and what you should be paying is growing every day. Here's what a proactive approach actually looks like.

news.title: How MRD Testing Is Reshaping Breast Cancer Risk Assessment
news.summary: New B-59 data shows circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA) and molecular residual disease (MRD) testing can better predict recurrence risk and help guide more personalized care in TNBC.

news.title: Orchestrating Affordability: The Critical New Role of the Health Plan
news.summary: Affordability won’t come from being a passive payer. See how health plans can connect networks, benefits, and experience to lower total cost.

news.title: A deeper dive into the ACCESS Model—Who’s participating, potential headwinds and how it could spur health plan adoption
news.summary: Many of the 150 digital health companies tapped to participate in the first cohort of the ACCESS model are bullish that it marks an inflection point for connected care and a way to prove out that AI-enabled medical services can move the needle on cost and quality.

news.title: Cleveland Clinic taps startup Luminai to test how AI can run hospital operations
news.summary: As the Cleveland Clinic works with Luminai, tech leadership sees opportunities to use its tech to tackle other administrative workflows, especially in workflows that are high-volume, complex and operationally intensive, Rohit Chandra, Cleveland Clinic chief digital officer, said.

news.title: Private Swiss biotechs hit record funding amid slowdown in public financing
news.summary: Funding for privately traded Swiss biotechs hit a new high last year, climbing 38% to 1.15 billion Swiss francs ($1.5 billion). However, a relatively small pool of companies drove the funding jump while many of their peers raised nothing, echoing the global gap between the haves and have-nots.

news.title: Vertex drops Moderna-partnered inhaled cystic fibrosis candidate after unresolved tolerability issues
news.summary: After pausing a trial for a Moderna-partnered cystic fibrosis candidate due to subpar tolerability, Vertex Pharmaceuticals has now decided to discontinue the program entirely.

news.title: Passage launches strategic review and 75% layoffs after gene therapy path blocked by FDA
news.summary: After some tough feedback from the FDA on the path forward for its lead gene therapy, Passage Bio has launched a strategic review and set out a plan to whittle down its headcount by 75%.

news.title: ‘A concerted industry-government effort’: Strand Therapeutics CEO Jake Becraft on FDA’s IND reform and China
news.summary: For Jake Becraft, Ph.D., the future of America’s biomedical innovation isn’t just playing out in the lab but is being shaped by industrial policy. In a recent interview with Fierce Biotech, the Strand Therapeutics CEO warned that the U.S. faces a critical choice: either modernize its regulatory framework for early-stage clinical trials or risk losing the capabilities permanently.

news.title: Takeda pivotal trial win improves convenience for PID patients relative to HyQvia
news.summary: A pivotal study of a Takeda primary immunodeficiency disease (PID) asset has hit its primary endpoint, demonstrating pharmacokinetic (PK) comparability between the asset and HyQvia, which was approved to treat PID in 2014.

news.title: Mirum maps FDA path after anti-itch candidate scores ph. 2 win
news.summary: Mirum Pharmaceuticals is moving toward an FDA filing after its small molecule volixibat significantly reduced severe itching in patients with a rare liver disease.

news.title: Windward catches $165M breeze to fuel long-acting TSLP ambitions
news.summary: Windward Bio is in full sail with a $165 million crossover financing to push its long-acting anti-TSLP antibody into phase 3, betting that a twice-yearly dosing schedule will be a key differentiator in an increasingly crowded respiratory market.

news.title: Celcuity’s gedatolisib beats Novartis’ Piqray in phase 3 breast cancer study
news.summary: A phase 3 trial of Celcuity’s pan-PI3K/mTOR inhibitor has hit its primary endpoint, clearing the biotech to file a second submission for a molecule already under review by the FDA.

news.title: Scaling theranostics through access, safety and trust
news.summary: Curium’s Mike Patterson on improving access, safety and trust to scale theranostics and enhance patient care.

news.title: UCB inks $2B Candid buyout to join Gilead in autoimmune field
news.summary: UCB has struck a deal to acquire Candid Therapeutics for $2 billion upfront, hijacking a planned reverse merger with Rallybio to challenge Gilead for an emerging opportunity for T-cell engagers.

news.title: ‘Real momentum’ or ‘completely absurd’? How Trump’s support has shaken up psychedelics
news.summary: The Trump administration's support could launch psychedelics across the regulatory finish line or doom the field to perpetual questions of legitimacy.

news.title: Nuvalent teams up with Guardant Health for companion diagnostics
news.summary: Massachusetts biotech Nuvalent is turning to Guardant Health to develop companion diagnostics for its targeted cancer drugs, signing a multi-year deal to tap into Guardant’s “Infinity” platform.

papers.title: PhysicianBench: Evaluating LLM Agents in Real-World EHR Environments
papers.summary: We introduce PhysicianBench, a benchmark for evaluating LLM agents on physician tasks grounded in real clinical setting within electronic health record (EHR) environments. Existing medical agent benchmarks primarily focus on static knowledge recall, single-step atomic actions, or action intent without verifiable execution against the environment. As a result, they fail to capture the long-horizon, composite workflows that characterize real clinical systems. PhysicianBench comprises 100 long-horizon tasks adapted from real consultation cases between primary care and subspecialty physicians, wit

papers.title: Safety Drift After Fine-Tuning: Evidence from High-Stakes Domains
papers.summary: Foundation models are routinely fine-tuned for use in particular domains, yet safety assessments are typically conducted only on base models, implicitly assuming that safety properties persist through downstream adaptation. We test this assumption by analyzing the safety behavior of 100 models, including widely deployed fine-tunes in the medical and legal domains as well as controlled adaptations of open foundation models alongside their bases. Across general-purpose and domain-specific safety benchmarks, we find that benign fine-tuning induces large, heterogeneous, and often contradictory cha
